canonical_filename.mechanism: SKILL.md (fixed filename per Agent Skills open standard)

custom_filename.mechanism: not observed — Agent Skills standard uses fixed SKILL.md filename

description.mechanism: yaml frontmatter key: description (required per Agent Skills open standard)

display_name.mechanism: yaml frontmatter key: name (required per Agent Skills open standard)

project_scope.mechanism: skill directory under .github/skills/<name>/, .claude/skills/<name>/, or .agents/skills/<name>/ in project repository

shared_scope.mechanism: organization-level and enterprise-level skills explicitly stated as 'coming soon' in source

user_invocable.mechanism: slash-prefixed skill name in user prompt (e.g. /frontend-design)

providerExtensions.name: Supporting Files
providerExtensions.description: Skills are described as "folders of instructions, scripts, and resources" — the skill directory contains more than just SKILL.md. Additional files (scripts, resources, instructions) can be bundled alongside the main SKILL.md entrypoint. Copilot loads the skill "when relevant," implying the directory is a unit of loading, not just the single markdown file.

providerExtensions.name: Description-Based Auto-Invocation
providerExtensions.description: Copilot loads skills "when relevant to improve its performance in specialized tasks." This implies description-driven automatic invocation — the model reads skill descriptions and activates relevant skills without an explicit user slash-command. This is distinct from the user_invocable slash-command syntax (/skill-name) which is explicit; the auto-invocation path is model-driven based on task relevance.

providerExtensions.name: Plugin Scope
providerExtensions.description: Skills can be bundled with and added via plugins. The `/skills remove` command explicitly distinguishes between skills added directly and skills added as part of a plugin — plugin skills must be managed through the plugin itself, not via `/skills remove`. The `/skills info` subcommand can identify which plugin a skill came from. Plugin-scoped skills occupy their own management namespace separate from directly-installed personal and project skills.

providerExtensions.name: CLI Skill Management Commands
providerExtensions.description: Copilot CLI exposes a dedicated `/skills` command with subcommands for managing the full skill lifecycle within a CLI session: `/skills list` lists currently available skills (also triggered by the natural language prompt "What skills do you have?"); `/skills` (bare) opens an interactive toggle UI using arrow keys and spacebar to enable/disable individual skills; `/skills info` shows details about a skill including its disk location and which plugin it came from; `/skills add` adds an alternative directory location for storing skills; `/skills reload` loads a newly added skill without restarting the CLI session; `/skills remove SKILL-DIRECTORY` removes a directly-added skill (not plugin skills). This CLI management surface is unique to Copilot CLI and has no equivalent in other providers' skills implementations.
